rule.name: La Paz
rule.message: The city name La Paz needs to be capitalized.

rule.name: bay area (Bay Area)
rule.message: Capitalize this word if you mean the region in California.

rule.name: 'breath of scope' (breadth of scope)
rule.message: Did you mean breadth?

rule.name: Unite Kingdom -> United Kingdom
rule.message: Did you mean United Kingdom?

rule.name: were're → we're
rule.message: Did you mean we\2?

rule.name: t he (the)
rule.message: Did you mean the?

rule.name: want be (won't be)
rule.message: Did you mean won't \2 or want to \2?

rule.name: Ive -> I've
rule.message: Possible spelling mistake found.

rule.name: re cent (recent)
rule.message: Did you mean recent?

rule.name: if which (if, which)
rule.message: One of these words is redundant, or a comma is missing.

rule.name: lot's of (lots of)
rule.message: Did you mean lots?

rule.name: ah ha (aha)
rule.message: This word is usually spelled as one word.

rule.name: a was (way)
rule.message: Did you mean way?

rule.name: as oppose to (as opposed to)
rule.message: Did you mean the common phrase/idiom as opposed to?

rule.name: apart form (apart from)
rule.message: Possible typo. Did you mean apart from?

rule.name: up to data (date)
rule.message: Did you mean date?

rule.name: feel tree (free) to
rule.message: Did you mean free?

rule.name: de juro (jure)
rule.message: This is an incorrect spelling of a Latin phrase. Did you mean de jure (according to law)?

rule.name: all be it (albeit)
rule.message: Did you mean albeit (=although)?

rule.name: all over the word (all over the world)
rule.message: Did you mean \1 \2 \3 world (=globally)?

rule.name: there exits (there exists)
rule.message: Did you mean exists?

rule.name: in parenthesis (in parentheses)
rule.message: Did you mean in parentheses? 'parenthesis' is the singular.

rule.name: in stead of (instead of)
rule.message: Did you mean \1\2 \3?

rule.name: I've go to (I've got to)
rule.message: Did you mean got?

rule.name: for along time (for a long time)
rule.message: Did you mean for a long time?

rule.name: away fro (away from)
rule.message: Did you mean away from?

rule.name: fro (for)
rule.message: Did you mean for or from?

rule.name: last but not last (least)
rule.message: Possible typo. Did you mean: \1 \2 \3 least?

rule.name: one in the same (one and the same)
rule.message: Did you mean one and the same?

rule.name: per say (per se)
rule.message: Did you mean per se (=by itself, by themselves)?

rule.name: some what (somewhat)
rule.message: Did you mean \1\2 (= slightly)?

rule.name: under wear (underwear)
rule.message: Did you mean underwear (=underclothes)?

rule.name: whatsoever → whatsoever
rule.message: Did you mean whatsoever?

rule.name: without out (without)
rule.message: Did you mean without?

rule.name: tot he (to the)
rule.message: Did you mean to the?

rule.name: with out (without)
rule.message: This word is usually spelled as one word. Did you mean without?

rule.name: allot of (a lot of)
rule.message: Did you mean a lot of (=plenty)?

rule.name: is were (where)
rule.message: Did you mean where?

rule.name: see (sea)
rule.message: Did you mean sea or seeing?

rule.name: through (throw) away
rule.message: Did you mean throw?

rule.name: my (may) be
rule.message: Did you mean may be or might be?

rule.name: can not (cannot)
rule.message: Did you mean cannot?

rule.name: doe snot (does not)
rule.message: Did you mean \1s ?

rule.name: missing apostrophe in 'Presidents Day'
rule.message: It appears that a possessive apostrophe is missing, if you mean the legal holiday Presidents' Day.

rule.name: missing apostrophe in 'International Workers Day'
rule.message: It appears that a possessive apostrophe is missing, if you mean the legal holiday International Workers' Day.

rule.name: missing apostrophe in 'Childrens Day'
rule.message: It appears that a possessive apostrophe is missing.

rule.name: very match (much)
rule.message: Did you mean very much?

rule.name: ...you vary (very) much
rule.message: Did you mean very?

rule.name: all ways (always)
rule.message: Did you mean always?

rule.name: self fish (selfish)
rule.message: Did you mean the adjective selfish (spelled as one word)?

rule.name: every where (everywhere)
rule.message: Did you mean everywhere?

rule.name: how ever (however)
rule.message: Did you mean however?

rule.name: laughing stock (laughingstock)
rule.message: Did you mean laughingstock?

rule.name: mean while (meanwhile)
rule.message: Did you mean meanwhile?

rule.name: nation wide (nationwide)
rule.message: Did you mean nationwide?

rule.name: near by (nearby)
rule.message: Did you mean nearby?

rule.name: now a days (nowadays)
rule.message: Did you mean nowadays?

rule.name: now-a-days (nowadays)
rule.message: Did you mean nowadays?

rule.name: now-a-day (nowadays)
rule.message: Did you mean nowadays?

rule.name: out side (outside)
rule.message: Did you mean outside?

rule.name: over looked (overlooked)
rule.message: Did you mean overlooked?

rule.name: over looking (overlooking)
rule.message: Did you mean overlooking?

rule.name: over rated (overrated)
rule.message: Did you mean overrated?

rule.name: sky diving (skydiving)
rule.message: Did you mean skydiving?

rule.name: some how (somehow)
rule.message: Did you mean somehow?

rule.name: them selves (themselves)
rule.message: Did you mean themselves?

rule.name: world wide (worldwide)
rule.message: The adjective/adverb worldwide is spelled as one word.

rule.name: worth while (worthwhile)
rule.message: Did you mean worthwhile?

rule.name: way side (wayside)
rule.message: Did you mean wayside?

rule.name: along side (alongside)
rule.message: Did you mean alongside?

rule.name: be cause (because)
rule.message: Did you mean because?

rule.name: be ware (beware)
rule.message: Did you mean beware?

rule.name: before hand (beforehand)
rule.message: Did you mean beforehand?

rule.name: through out (throughout)
rule.message: Did you mean throughout?

rule.name: on-going (ongoing)
rule.message: Did you mean ongoing?

rule.name: free lance (freelance)
rule.message: Did you mean freelance?

rule.name: free lances (freelances)
rule.message: Did you mean freelances?

rule.name: free lancing (freelancing)
rule.message: Did you mean freelancing?

rule.name: key words (keywords)
rule.message: Did you mean keywords?

rule.name: crowd sourcing (crowdsourcing)
rule.message: Did you mean crowdsourcing?

rule.name: meta data (metadata)
rule.message: Did you mean metadata?

rule.name: past time (pastime)
rule.message: Did you mean pastime?

rule.name: any where (anywhere)
rule.message: Did you mean anywhere?

rule.name: some where (somewhere)
rule.message: Did you mean somewhere?

rule.name: other then (other than)
rule.message: Did you mean other than?

rule.name: baited (bated) breath
rule.message: Did you mean bated breath?

rule.name: bare (bear) in mind
rule.message: Did you mean bear in mind?

rule.name: playoff birth (berth)
rule.message: Did you mean playoff berth?

rule.name: disc break (brakes)
rule.message: Did you mean disc brake?

rule.name: hit the breaks (brakes)
rule.message: Did you mean hit the brakes?

rule.name: full compliment (complement) of
rule.message: Did you mean full complement of?

rule.name: in edition (addition) to
rule.message: Did you mean in addition to?

rule.name: faired (fared) as well
rule.message: Did you mean fared as well?

rule.name: and so fourth (forth)
rule.message: Did you mean and so forth?

rule.name: forth (fourth) place
rule.message: Did you mean fourth place?

rule.name: door jam (jamb)
rule.message: Did you mean door jamb?

rule.name: mute (moot) point
rule.message: Did you mean moot point?

rule.name: managerial reigns (reins)
rule.message: Did you mean managerial reins?

rule.name: reigns (reins) of power
rule.message: Did you mean reins of power?

rule.name: free reign (rein)
rule.message: Did you mean free rein?

rule.name: role (roll) call
rule.message: Did you mean roll call?

rule.name: construction sight (site)
rule.message: Did you mean construction site?

rule.name: out of sink (sync)
rule.message: Did you mean out of sync?

rule.name: different tact (tack)
rule.message: Did you mean different tack?

rule.name: waived (waved) off
rule.message: Did you mean waved off?

rule.name: wreck (wreak) havoc
rule.message: Did you mean wreak havoc?

rule.name: duck (duct) tape
rule.message: Did you mean duct?

rule.name: oft he (of the)
rule.message: Did you mean of the?

rule.name: hast o (has to)
rule.message: Did you mean has to?

rule.name: laid (lay) ahead
rule.message: Did you mean lay ahead?

rule.name: jive (jibe) with
rule.message: Make sure that '\1' refers to a type of dance. Did you mean jibe with?

rule.name: mash (mashed) potatoes
rule.message: Did you mean mashed potatoes?

rule.name: is renown (renowned) for
rule.message: Did you mean is renowned for?

rule.name: brew haha (brouhaha)
rule.message: Did you mean brouhaha?

rule.name: working (work in) progress
rule.message: Did you mean work in progress?

rule.name: historic (historical) record
rule.message: Did you mean historical record?

rule.name: guest stared (guest-starred)
rule.message: Did you mean guest-starred?

rule.name: is contained of (contains)
rule.message: Did you mean contains?

rule.name: after been (being)
rule.message: Did you mean after being?

rule.name: imminent (eminent) domain
rule.message: Did you mean eminent domain?

rule.name: chomping (champing) at the bit
rule.message: Did you mean champing at the bit?

rule.name: constellation (consolation) prize
rule.message: Did you mean consolation prize?

rule.name: constitutes (consists) of
rule.message: Did you mean consists of?

rule.name: could (couldn't) care less
rule.message: Did you mean couldn't care less?

rule.name: could (couldn't) give a damn
rule.message: Did you mean couldn't give a damn?

rule.name: daily regiment (regimen)
rule.message: Did you mean daily regimen?

rule.name: de factor (facto)
rule.message: Did you mean de facto?

rule.name: worse-case (worst-case) scenario
rule.message: Did you mean worst-case scenario?

rule.name: worst (worse) comes to worst
rule.message: Did you mean worse comes to worst?

rule.name: treasure trough (trove)
rule.message: Did you mean treasure trove?

rule.name: ad nauseum (nauseam)
rule.message: Did you mean ad nauseam?

rule.name: land lover (landlubber)
rule.message: Did you mean landlubber?

rule.name: strike a cord (chord)
rule.message: Did you mean strike a chord?

rule.name: safety (safe) deposit box
rule.message: Did you mean safe deposit box?

rule.name: statue (statute) of limitations
rule.message: Did you mean statute of limitations?

rule.name: all the farther (as far as)
rule.message: Did you mean as far as?

rule.name: discussions around (about)
rule.message: Did you mean about?

rule.name: as time progressed (passed)
rule.message: Did you mean as time passed?

rule.name: as follow (follows)
rule.message: Did you mean as follows?

rule.name: beyond the pail (pale)
rule.message: Did you mean beyond the pale?

rule.name: beckon (beck and) call
rule.message: Did you mean beck and call?

rule.name: begs (beggars) belief
rule.message: Did you mean beggars belief?

rule.name: buttload (boatload)
rule.message: Did you mean boatload?

rule.name: build off of (build on)
rule.message: Did you mean build on?

rule.name: trite (tried) and true
rule.message: Did you mean tried and true?

rule.name: between you and I (me)
rule.message: Did you mean between you and me?

rule.name: every (ever) since
rule.message: Did you mean ever since?

rule.name: fowl (fell) swoop
rule.message: Did you mean fell swoop?

rule.name: hardly never (ever)
rule.message: Did you mean hardly ever?

rule.name: hew (hue) and cry
rule.message: Did you mean hue and cry?

rule.name: minus well (might as well)
rule.message: Did you mean might as well?

rule.name: oft chance (off chance)
rule.message: Did you mean off chance?

rule.name: on the same token (by the same token)
rule.message: Did you mean by the same token?

rule.name: one of the only (few)
rule.message: Some people suggest that the phrase is idiomatic or illogical and that a better option is one of the few.

rule.name: pedal to the medal (metal)
rule.message: Did you mean pedal to the metal?

rule.name: pawn off (palm off)
rule.message: Did you mean palm off?

rule.name: plays a factor (plays a role)
rule.message: Did you mean plays a role?

rule.name: realms (realm) of possibility
rule.message: Did you mean realm of possibility?

rule.name: ring (wring) its neck
rule.message: Did you mean wring its neck?

rule.name: souse (sous) chef
rule.message: Did you mean sous chef?

rule.name: stock and trade (stock in trade)
rule.message: Did you mean stock in trade?

rule.name: sound byte (bite)
rule.message: Did you mean sound bite?

rule.name: to the manor (manner) born
rule.message: Did you mean to the manner born?

rule.name: vintage (vantage) point
rule.message: Did you mean vantage point?

rule.name: whim (wing) and a prayer
rule.message: Did you mean wing and a prayer?

rule.name: year end (in) and year out
rule.message: Did you mean year in and year out?

rule.name: hand and hand (hand in hand)
rule.message: Did you mean hand in hand?

rule.name: grasping for straws (grasping at straws)
rule.message: Did you mean grasping at straws?

rule.name: grill (grilled) cheese
rule.message: Did you mean grilled cheese?

rule.name: might has (as) well
rule.message: Did you mean might as well?

rule.name: on the contraire (au contraire)
rule.message: Did you mean au contraire or on the contrary?

rule.name: damp squid (squib)
rule.message: Did you mean damp squib?

rule.name: cursing (coursing) through veins
rule.message: Did you mean coursing through veins?

rule.name: daring-do (derring-do)
rule.message: Did you mean derring-do?

rule.name: death nail (knell)
rule.message: Did you mean death knell?

rule.name: reap what you sew (sow)
rule.message: Did you mean reap what you sow?

rule.name: spare (spur) of the moment
rule.message: Did you mean spur of the moment?

rule.name: tongue and (in) cheek
rule.message: Did you mean tongue in cheek?

rule.name: urine analysis (urinalysis)
rule.message: Did you mean urinalysis?

rule.name: wet (whet) your appetite
rule.message: Did you mean whet your appetite?

rule.name: tattle-tail (tattle-tale)
rule.message: Did you mean tattle-tale?

rule.name: on the lamb (lam)
rule.message: Did you mean on the lam?

rule.name: Im am (I am)
rule.message: Did you mean I am?

rule.name: flashpoint (flash point)
rule.message: Did you mean flash point?

rule.name: can blackout (black out)
rule.message: Did you mean can black out?

rule.name: got shutout (shut out)
rule.message: Did you mean got shut out?

rule.name: in along (a long) time
rule.message: Did you mean in a long time?

rule.name: incase (in case) of
rule.message: Did you mean in case of?

rule.name: Portland Trailblazers (Trail Blazers)
rule.message: Did you mean Portland Trail Blazers?

rule.name: take awhile (a while)
rule.message: Did you mean take a while?

rule.name: to backout (back out)
rule.message: Did you mean to back out?

rule.name: along (a long) time
rule.message: Did you mean a long time?

rule.name: downpayment (down payment)
rule.message: Did you mean down payment?

rule.name: ala mode (a la mode)
rule.message: Did you mean à la mode?

rule.name: afterall (after all)
rule.message: Did you mean after all?

rule.name: Th (The/thorium)
rule.message: Make sure that 'Th' is the chemical symbol for thorium, and not a typing error. Did you mean The?

rule.name: hi (his, high) time
rule.message: Did you mean high \2 or his \2?

rule.name: Alzheimers
rule.message: The name of this disease is always capitalized and spelled with a possessive apostrophe.

rule.name: a god send (godsend)
rule.message: This noun is normally spelled as one word.

rule.name: missing hyphen in 'drop dead'
rule.message: The adjective or adverb \1-\2 is normally spelled with a hyphen.

rule.name: decision making (decision-making)
rule.message: The noun \1-\2 (= the process of deciding something) is spelled with a hyphen.

rule.name: Christmas time (Christmastime)
rule.message: The noun Christmastime (= the Christmas season) is normally spelled as one word.

rule.name: AstraZeneca
rule.message: The pharma company AstraZeneca is spelled without a space in between.

rule.name: note taking (note-taking)
rule.message: The noun or adjective \1-\2 is normally spelled with a hyphen.

rule.name: attorney generals (attorneys general)
rule.message: The set phrase "attorney general" is correctly pluralized as attorneys general.

rule.name: He has much (a lot of) money
rule.message: In this context, a lot of or so much is more likely.

rule.name: to worried (worry) about
rule.message: Did you mean worry?

rule.name: with (the) exception of
rule.message: It appears that an article is missing in this idiom.

rule.name: I don't know how to (*missing verb*) it
rule.message: A verb seems to be missing between 'to' and 'it'.

rule.name: She is good-educated (well-educated).
rule.message: In English, we use "well" to modify participles and adjectives like "educated." Did you mean well-educated?

rule.name: How's the whether (weather)?
rule.message: Possible error detected.

rule.name: ...the situation in the (*omit the*) Ukraine
rule.message: Since becoming an independent nation in 1991, the article 'the' has been dropped from the name of the country of Ukraine.

rule.name: on the look out (lookout)
rule.message: In this idiom, lookout is spelled as one word.

rule.name: Wien vs Vienna
rule.message: The English name for this Austrian city is Vienna.

rule.name: Bayern vs Bavaria
rule.message: The English name for this German state is Bavaria.

rule.name: wolfs vs wolves
rule.message: The plural of the word "wolf" (= animal) is wolves.

rule.name: missing apostrophe in 'seasons greetings'
rule.message: This idiom is spelled with a possessive apostrophe.

rule.name: softwares → pieces of software
rule.message: In standard English, the noun "\1" is uncountable. Consider using an alternative.

rule.name: hardwares → pieces of hardware
rule.message: In standard English, the noun "\1" is uncountable. Consider using an alternative.

rule.name: hard working (hardworking)
rule.message: This adjective is spelled with a hyphen or as one word.

rule.name: under cover (undercover)
rule.message: The adjective undercover is spelled as one word if you mean it in the context of "undercover agent".

rule.name: Miami-Dade
rule.message: The name of this US county is spelled with a hyphen.

rule.name: Miami-Dade
rule.message: The name of this motorcycle maker is spelled with a hyphen.

rule.name: other wise (otherwise)
rule.message: This has to be spelled as one word.

rule.name: missing hyphen in 'off key'
rule.message: The adjective or adverb \1-\2 (= out of tune) is spelled with a hyphen

rule.name: Aircrafts
rule.message: The plural of "" is not "\1" but .

rule.name: splitted (split)
rule.message: The past tense and past participle of the verb "to split" is split. Alternatively you could use separated or divided.

rule.name: choosed (chose)
rule.message: The past tense and past participle of the verb "to choose" is chose. The past participle is chosen.

rule.name: bidded (bid)
rule.message: The past tense of the verb "to bid" is bid.

rule.name: striked (struck)
rule.message: The past tense or the past participle of the verb "to strike" is struck.

rule.name: casted (cast)
rule.message: The past tense and past participle of the verb "cast" is cast.

rule.name: bended (bent)
rule.message: The past tense and past participle of the verb "bend" is bent.

rule.name: many time (times)
rule.message: Did you mean \1 times or much time?

rule.name: proper capitalization of 'State of the Union'
rule.message: Consider using the proper capitalization if you mean the president's annual speech.

rule.name: getted (got)
rule.message: Did you mean got or get it?

rule.name: can able to (can)
rule.message: One of these words may be redundant.

rule.name: have having → have been having
rule.message: Did you mean been having?

rule.name: the (they, there) are
rule.message: Did you mean they \2 or there \2?

rule.name: Ya'll (Y'all)
rule.message: Did you mean y'all?

rule.name: very thanks (thanks a lot)
rule.message: Did you mean thanks a lot or many thanks?

rule.name: congratulations for (on)
rule.message: The correct preposition here is on.

rule.name: as will (well) as
rule.message: Did you mean as well as?

rule.name: is own
rule.message: Did you mean its \2?

rule.name: he dident (didn't)
rule.message: Did you mean didn't?

rule.name: I em (am)
rule.message: Did you mean I am?

rule.name: equally as (equally)
rule.message: Use either equally or as on its own. When comparing two nouns, use just as.

rule.name: if or not (whether or not)
rule.message: This is a non-standard phrase. Did you mean whether or not?

rule.name: Regarding to (regard to/regarding)
rule.message: The phrase '\1 \2' is not correct. Use or regard .

rule.name: shoe-in (shoo)
rule.message: Did you mean shoo-in?

rule.name: In the recent years (in recent years)
rule.message: The definite article "the" is not necessary in this context.

rule.name: Wrong phrase: 'in nowadays' (nowadays)
rule.message: \2 is used without 'in'.

rule.name: Wrong phrase: 'yesterday night' (last night)
rule.message: This is a non-standard collocation. Normally, last night is used.

rule.name: Wrong phrase: 'today night' (tonight)
rule.message: This is a non-standard collocation. Normally, tonight is used instead.

rule.name: Collocation: Popular among/with
rule.message: The usual collocation for "popular" is "with", not "among".

rule.name: Collocation: addiction to/of
rule.message: The usual preposition for "\1" is "to". Did you mean \1 to \3?

rule.name: Collocation: in/at a party
rule.message: Unless you mean "in a political party", the usual preposition for a party is "at".

rule.name: Collocation: damage of/to
rule.message: The usual collocation for the word "damage" is "to", not "of".

rule.name: Collocation: vulnerable from/to
rule.message: The usual collocation for the word "vulnerable" is "to", not "from", as in "vulnerable to attack".

rule.name: Collocation: perspectives about/on
rule.message: The usual preposition for "perspectives" is "on", not "about".

rule.name: Collocation: varieties in/of
rule.message: The usual preposition for "varieties" is "of", not "in".

rule.name: Collocation: far of/from
rule.message: The usual preposition for "far" is "from".

rule.name: Collocation: near from/to
rule.message: The usual preposition for "near" is "to".

rule.name: Collocation: fed up of/with
rule.message: The usual preposition for "\1 \2" is "with". Did you mean \1 \2 with?

rule.name: Collocation: ashamed from/of
rule.message: The usual collocation for "\1" is "of", not "\2". Did you mean \1 of?

rule.name: Collocation: afraid from/of
rule.message: The usual collocation for "\1" is "of", not "\2". Did you mean \1 of?

rule.name: Collocation: angry against/with
rule.message: The usual collocation for "\1" is "with" or "at", not "\2". Did you mean \1 with or \1 at?

rule.name: Collocation: careful for/with
rule.message: The usual collocation for "\1" is "with", not "\2". Did you mean \1 with?

rule.name: Collocation: full with/of
rule.message: The usual collocation for "\1" is "of", not "\2". Did you mean \1 of?

rule.name: Collocation: tired about/of
rule.message: The usual collocation for "\1" is "of" or "from", not "\2". Did you mean tired of or tired from?

rule.name: Collocation: advertisement of/for
rule.message: The usual collocation for "\1" is "for", not "\2". Did you mean advertisement for?

rule.name: Disappointed with, at, or by
rule.message: The usual collocation for "disappointed" is "with", "at", "by", but never "of". Did you mean \1 with?

rule.name: Collocation: analysis about/of
rule.message: The usual collocation for "analysis" is "of".

rule.name: Collocation: motivation in/to
rule.message: Do not use "motivation in \3". Use "to" + infinitive form instead.

rule.name: Missing comma 'first come first serve'
rule.message: It seems that a comma is missing.

rule.name: Missing hyphen: face first (face-first)
rule.message: Did you mean the adverb \1-\2?

rule.name: Mabey vs. Maybe
rule.message: "Mabey" is a common surname. Did you mean maybe (= perhaps)?

rule.name: It's an allusion (illusion) of not being lonely
rule.message: In most contexts, you allude "to" something. Did you mean to write "illusion" here?

rule.name: Japanise vs Japanese
rule.message: Did you mean Japanese (= people from Japan)?

rule.name: sigh vs sight
rule.message: Did you mean the noun sight?

rule.name: dod vs did
rule.message: Did you mean did \2?

rule.name: happed vs happened
rule.message: Did you mean happened or happen?

rule.name: once a (or) twice
rule.message: Did you mean or?

rule.name: price sensibility (sensitivity)
rule.message: Did you mean price sensitivity (= degree to which the price of a product affects consumers' purchasing behaviors)?

rule.name: zero-sum gain (game)
rule.message: Did you mean \1 game?

rule.name: marriage (wedding) anniversary
rule.message: Wedding anniversaries are the proper term to describe the anniversary of the day of a wedding.

rule.name: a blessing in the skies (disguise)
rule.message: Did you mean disguise (a blessing in disguise = an unfortunate event that results in a positive outcome)?

rule.name: one foul (fell) swoop
rule.message: Did you mean fell?

rule.name: doggy-dog (dog-eat-dog)
rule.message: Did you mean dog-eat-dog?

rule.name: intensive purposes (intents and purposes)
rule.message: Did you mean intents and \3?

rule.name: from than (then) on
rule.message: Did you mean from then on?

rule.name: ..., than, ... (then)
rule.message: Did you mean then?

rule.name: who than (then)
rule.message: Did you mean then?

rule.name: in the passed (in the past)
rule.message: Did you mean in the past?

rule.name: witch haunt (witch hunt)
rule.message: Did you mean \1 hunt (=search for witches)?

rule.name: sneak peak (sneak peek)
rule.message: Did you mean \1 peek (=preview)?

rule.name: another (in other)
rule.message: Did you mean in other words?

rule.name: sobber (sober)
rule.message: Did you mean sober (=not intoxicated)? "Sobber" means "weeper, someone one who cries".

rule.name: in vein (in vain)
rule.message: Did you mean in vain (=unsuccessfully)?

rule.name: insure that (ensure that)
rule.message: Did you mean ensure (=make sure)? 'Insure' means 'pay money to insurance company'.

rule.name: in masse (en masse)
rule.message: Did you mean en masse?

rule.name: en mass (en masse)
rule.message: Did you mean en masse?

rule.name: for sell (for sale)
rule.message: Did you mean for sale?

rule.name: chalk full (chock-full)
rule.message: Did you mean chock-full?

rule.name: egg yoke (egg yolk)
rule.message: Did you mean egg yolk?

rule.name: et all (et al.)
rule.message: Did you mean et al. (=and others) or at all?

rule.name: bye the way (by the way)
rule.message: Did you mean by the way?

rule.name: back and fourth (back and forth)
rule.message: Did you mean back and forth?

rule.name: back in forth (back and forth)
rule.message: Did you mean back and forth?

rule.name: formally known as (formerly known as)
rule.message: Did you mean formerly \2 \3 (=known earlier under the name of)?

rule.name: 'Do to that fact that' → 'Due to'
rule.message: Use due to or because of to fix spelling and readability of this sentence.

rule.name: Dependent
rule.message: Did you mean dependent on?

rule.name: Further ado instead of further adieu
rule.message: Did you mean further ado?

rule.name: Adapt to v. adopt to
rule.message: Did you mean adapt to?

rule.name: Aide in wrong context
rule.message: Did you mean to aid?

rule.name: 'all the further' is a common, but incorrect phrase
rule.message: Did you mean as far as?

rule.name: Amendable to error
rule.message: Did you mean amenable?

rule.name: Ascetic acid v acetic acid
rule.message: Did you mean acetic acid?

rule.name: Missing 'as' in 'as well as or better than'
rule.message: Did you mean as well as or better than?

rule.name: Awaiting for - incorrect
rule.message: Did you mean awaiting or waiting for?

rule.name: deep-seeded (deep-seated)
rule.message: Did you mean deep-seated?

rule.name: calender (calendar)
rule.message: Did you mean calendar, a system of organizing days? A 'calender' is a machine.

rule.name: on behave (on behalf)
rule.message: Did you mean on behalf (in the name of, as a representative of)?

rule.name: wildly (widely) accepted
rule.message: Did you mean widely?

rule.name: ease off (of) use
rule.message: Did you mean of?

rule.name: admin (admit)
rule.message: Did you mean admit (=confess)?

rule.name: along the same vein (along the same line, in the same vein)
rule.message: This phrase is nonstandard. Use in the same vein or along the same line.

rule.name: Misused phrase: 'ever so often (every so often)'
rule.message: This phrase is non-standard in most writing. Did you mean every \2 \3?

rule.name: early adapter (early adopter)
rule.message: Did you mean early adopter (=someone who takes something early on)?

rule.name: far be it for me (far be it from me)
rule.message: Did you mean far be it from me (=may this possibility be far away from me)?

rule.name: money is no option (money is no object)
rule.message: Did you mean money is no object (=money is no obstacle, cost what it may)?

rule.name: nip it in the butt (nip it in the bud)
rule.message: Did you mean nip it in the bud (=stop it from flowering completely)?

rule.name: now and days (nowadays)
rule.message: Did you mean nowadays?

rule.name: the proof is in the pudding (the proof of the pudding is in the eating)
rule.message: If you don't want to look for proofs hidden in your pudding, you probably meant the proof of the pudding is in the eating.

rule.name: qui bono (cui bono)
rule.message: Non-standard Latin. Did you mean cui bono (=to whose benefit)?

rule.name: very known (very well-known, well-known)
rule.message: Incorrect phrase. Use very well-known or well-known instead.

rule.name: legal term: informations
rule.message: The word '\1' is a legal term. In standard English, the word 'information' is a non-count noun.

rule.name: right in (up) my alley
rule.message: Did you mean 'up'?

rule.name: two peas in a pot (pod)
rule.message: Did you mean 'pod'?

rule.name: tender hooks (tenterhooks)
rule.message: Did you mean 'tenterhooks'?

rule.name: case and (in) point
rule.message: Did you mean 'case in point'?

rule.name: a la (à la)
rule.message: ‘A la’ is a foreign expression which originally has a diacritic.

rule.name: SpaceX
rule.message: Did you mean SpaceX (= aerospace company)?

rule.name: PowerShell
rule.message: Did you mean PowerShell (= task automation from Microsoft)?

rule.name: Gmail
rule.message: Did you mean Gmail (= email client from Google)?

rule.name: WhatsApp
rule.message: Did you mean WhatsApp (= Messenger) or what's up (= question)?

rule.name: Chat GPT (ChatGPT)
rule.message: ChatGPT (= Artificial Intelligence) is spelled as one word.

rule.name: Eifel (Eiffel)
rule.message: Did you mean the Eiffel Tower in Paris?

rule.name: GitHub
rule.message: Did you mean GitHub (= Coding Platform)?

rule.name: Air Bnb (Airbnb)
rule.message: The name of this online marketplace is spelled as one word.

rule.name: Call of Duty
rule.message: Did you mean the video game Call of Duty?

rule.name: T-rex (T. rex)
rule.message: The official abbreviation for "Tyrannosaurus rex" is T. rex.

rule.name: JavaScript
rule.message: Did you mean JavaScript (= programming language)?

rule.name: PayPal
rule.message: The name (= payment provider) is spelled as one word.

rule.name: Coca Cola (Coca-Cola)
rule.message: The name of this popular soft drink is capitalized and spelled with a hyphen.

rule.name: Coldplay
rule.message: Did you mean Coldplay (= music band)?

rule.name: Saint Tropez (Saint-Tropez)
rule.message: The name of this town on the French Riviera is spelled with a hyphen.

rule.name: Rolls Royce (Rolls-Royce)
rule.message: This car brand is normally spelled with a hyphen Rolls-Royce.

rule.name: Jay Z (Jay-Z)
rule.message: The name of this rapper is spelled with a hyphen Jay-Z.

rule.name: Objective-C
rule.message: Did you mean the programming language Objective-C?

rule.name: McDonald's
rule.message: Did you mean the fast food chain McDonald's (always spelled with a possessive apostrophe)?

rule.name: Sam's Club
rule.message: Did you mean the retail warehouse Sam's Club (always spelled with a possessive apostrophe)?

rule.name: Lockheed Martin
rule.message: Did you mean the defense company Lockheed Martin?

rule.name: Uncle Bens -> Ben's
rule.message: Did you mean the rice brand Uncle Ben's (capitalized and spelled with a possessive apostrophe)?

rule.name: Wendy's
rule.message: Did you mean the name Wendy's (spelled with a possessive apostrophe)?

rule.name: Sainsbury's
rule.message: Did you mean the grocery shopping company Sainsbury's (spelled with a possessive apostrophe)?

rule.name: Erdogan (Erdoğan)
rule.message: The name of the Turkish politician is spelled Erdoğan (= Recep Tayyip Erdoğan).

rule.name: Jimmy Buffet (Buffett)
rule.message: Did you mean Jimmy Buffett?

rule.name: Harison (Harrison) Ford
rule.message: Did you mean the American actor and film producer Harrison Ford?

rule.name: Jimmy (Jimi) Hendrix
rule.message: Did you mean Jimi Hendrix?

rule.name: Phillips (Philips) Arena
rule.message: Did you mean Philips Arena?

rule.name: AppStore (App Store, Amazon Appstore)
rule.message: If you mean the Apple digital distribution platform, use App Store. For Amazon, use Amazon Appstore.

rule.name: Pokémon
rule.message: The name of this game or TV show is spelled with an accent.

rule.name: Smart ellipsis (…)
rule.message: Consider using the typographical ellipsis character here instead.

rule.name: busses (buses)
rule.message: The more common plural form of "bus" (= motor vehicle) is buses.

rule.name: anyways (anyway)
rule.message: The word '\1' is informal American English. Did you mean anyway?

rule.name: Thank you much
rule.message: This phrase is non-standard. Consider replacing it.

rule.name: used to could (be able to)
rule.message: Did you mean be able to?

rule.name: bestest (best)
rule.message: The word "\1" is informal.

rule.name: bored of (with)
rule.message: In standard English it's more accepted to use bored with or bored by.

rule.name: 'themself'
rule.message: Generally speaking, "themself" is only acceptable when referring to a singular entity (such as the singular usage of "they", which is the preferred pronoun for many non-binary people). If "themself" refers to a plural entity (such as "everybody", or the standard usage of "they"), you should use "themselves".

rule.name: 'ourself'
rule.message: The term is non-standard and not widely accepted.

rule.name: outta (out of)
rule.message: The word '\1' is informal.

rule.name: lemme (let me)
rule.message: The word '\1' is informal.

rule.name: an invite (invitation)
rule.message: The noun invitation is usually used instead of 'invite' in formal writing.

rule.name: irregardless (regardless)
rule.message: 'Irregardless' is nonstandard. In formal style you should use regardless.

rule.name: 'also' at the end of the sentence
rule.message: 'Also' is not used at the end of the sentence. Use as well or too instead.

rule.name: directly antithetical (antithetical)
rule.message: This phrase verges on redundancy. Consider writing .

rule.name: sufficient enough → sufficient
rule.message: 'Sufficient enough' is redundant. Consider using only sufficient.

rule.name: of any of → of
rule.message: Consider simply using of instead.

rule.name: because of the fact that (because)
rule.message: This phrase is redundant. Consider using because.

rule.name: and also (and/also)
rule.message: Consider using and or also.

rule.name: adequate enough (adequate)
rule.message: This phrase might be redundant. Consider using \1.

rule.name: are able to (can)
rule.message: This expression is wordy. Consider using can.

rule.name: as of yet (yet)
rule.message: Consider using yet or as yet.

rule.name: autobiography of a life (autobiography)
rule.message: Consider using \1 or story \2 \3 \4 instead.

rule.name: bald-headed (bald)
rule.message: Consider using bald.

rule.name: best ever (best)
rule.message: This phrase might be redundant. Consider using \1.

rule.name: temporary bivouac (bivouac)
rule.message: This phrase may be redundant. Consider writing .

rule.name: bivouac camp (bivouac)
rule.message: This phrase is redundant. Consider writing .

rule.name: bitter in taste (bitter)
rule.message: Consider using \1.

rule.name: accused of a charge (charged with)
rule.message: This phrase may be redundant. Consider writing: charged with instead.

rule.name: circumstances surrounding (circumstances of)
rule.message: This phrase is redundant. Consider writing of.

rule.name: each and every one (each one)
rule.message: Consider using \1 \4.

rule.name: right over (over)
rule.message: This phrase is redundant. Consider using \2.

rule.name: self-admitted (admitted)
rule.message: This phrase is redundant. Consider using just admitted.

rule.name: subject matter (subject)
rule.message: This phrase is redundant. Consider using \1 to avoid wordiness.

rule.name: so as to (to)
rule.message: 'So as to' expresses purpose and is used in formal texts. Consider using to.

rule.name: on a few occasions (occasionally)
rule.message: Consider using occasionally

rule.name: there are also other (also)
rule.message: Consider using there are other or there are also

rule.name: a small number of (a few)
rule.message: Specify a number, remove phrase, use a few, or use some

rule.name: subsequent to (after)
rule.message: Consider using after

rule.name: with the exception of (except)
rule.message: Consider using except or except for

rule.name: every now and then (now and then)
rule.message: Consider an alternative to strengthen your wording.

rule.name: established fact (fact)
rule.message: Consider using .

rule.name: salsa sauce (salsa)
rule.message: Consider using \1.

rule.name: originally born in (born in)
rule.message: Consider using \2 \3.

rule.name: will in the future (will)
rule.message: Consider using will or will eventually.

rule.name: work colleague (colleague)
rule.message: Consider using \2.

rule.name: honest truth (truth)
rule.message: Consider using \2.

rule.name: in order to (to)
rule.message: This phrase might be considered redundant. Consider using \3.

rule.name: point being is that (point is that/point being that)
rule.message: Consider using \1 \3 \4 or \1 \2 \4.

rule.name: reason why (reason)
rule.message: Consider using \2.

rule.name: so therefore (therefore)
rule.message: Consider using \1 or \2.

rule.name: therapeutic treatment (treatment)
rule.message: Consider using \2.

rule.name: rustic country (rustic)
rule.message: Consider using \1.

rule.name: Mount Fuji
rule.message: As “Yama” means “mountain” in Japanese, better write Mount Fuji?

rule.name: abundance
rule.message: Use enough or plenty, or give specific amount.

rule.name: accurate
rule.message: If it is not about aiming, use right; also detailed or correct.

rule.name: all things considered (omit)
rule.message: Remove as unnecessary.

rule.name: along the lines of (like)
rule.message: Did you mean like?

rule.name: as a matter of fact (in fact, actually, omit)
rule.message: Replace with less wordy in fact or actually, or remove completely.

rule.name: as per (as, in accordance with)
rule.message: Business jargon meaning in accordance with. Replace with as or modify the sentence.

rule.name: at all times (always)
rule.message: Simply, always. Pay attention to place in the sentence.

rule.name: at your earliest convenience (as soon as you can)
rule.message: Overly formal. Did you mean as soon as you can?

rule.name: wordy phrase: due to the fact
rule.message: Remove wordy "\1 \2 \3 \4 \5"

rule.name: belated (late)
rule.message: Outdated. Did you mean late?

rule.name: by means of (by, with, through)
rule.message: Did you mean by, with or through?

rule.name: currently
rule.message: Omit or replace with now

rule.name: for all intents and purposes (omit)
rule.message: See if you could remove this phrase.

rule.name: for the most part (omit)
rule.message: See if you could remove this phrase.

rule.name: for the purpose of (to, for)
rule.message: Did you mean to or for?

rule.name: in a manner of speaking (omit)
rule.message: See if you could remove this phrase.

rule.name: in a very real sense (omit)
rule.message: See if you could remove this phrase.

rule.name: in excess of (more than, over, exceeding)
rule.message: Better with more than, over, or exceeding

rule.name: in my opinion (omit)
rule.message: See if you could remove this phrase.

rule.name: in terms of (in, for)
rule.message: Change to in or for.

rule.name: in the affirmative (yes, affirmatively)
rule.message: Change to yes or affirmatively.

rule.name: in the case of (about, to)
rule.message: Change to about or to and change the word order of the sentence.

rule.name: in the nature of (like)
rule.message: Did you mean like?

rule.name: in the neighborhood of (about)
rule.message: Can be used as almost, approximately, more or less, around. Often about.

rule.name: in the process of
rule.message: Possibly empty phrase "\1 \2 \3 \4" when it means "currently".

rule.name: majority (most, usually) when not voting
rule.message: Majority in the sense of "more than half" is used with countable nouns only (esp. when voting); for uncountable nouns, use most or usually.

rule.name: not able (unable)
rule.message: Avoid using "not". Did you mean unable?

rule.name: not certain (uncertain)
rule.message: Avoid using "not". Did you mean uncertain?

rule.name: not unlike (similar, alike)
rule.message: Avoid using "not". Did you mean similar or alike?

rule.name: not many (few)
rule.message: Avoid using "not". Did you mean few?

rule.name: not the same (different)
rule.message: Avoid using "not". Did you mean different?

rule.name: on the occasion of (on, when, because of)
rule.message: Did you mean on? Try also when and because of.

rule.name: on the other hand (omit, but, yet)
rule.message: Avoid completely or try but, yet

rule.name: on two separate occasions (twice)
rule.message: Did you mean twice?

rule.name: sooner rather than later (soon, now)
rule.message: Try soon or now meaning sooner than expected.

rule.name: sufficient (enough)
rule.message: Use enough.

rule.name: that exists (omit)
rule.message: Try removing the phrase.

rule.name: the undersigned (I, me, we)
rule.message: Legalese. Did you mean I, me or we?

rule.name: type of (omit)
rule.message: Try removing the phrase "\1 \2".

rule.name: until such time as (until)
rule.message: Use until.

rule.name: you are requested (please)
rule.message: Replace with please

rule.name: Replace '#'
rule.message: Wikipedia style. Replace "#" with No. or number when referring to numbers or rankings.

rule.name: Replace '12 pm' with 'noon'
rule.message: Wikipedia style. Replace "12 pm" with noon for clarity.

rule.name: Replace '12 am' with 'midnight'
rule.message: Wikipedia style. Replace "12 am" with midnight for clarity.

rule.name: Replace 'currently' with a specific date
rule.message: Wikipedia style. Replace "currently" with a specific date, as "currently" will change over time.
